steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows "The keenest vision can't ___." The surrounding context describes the border between a leaf and its reflection as perfectly blended and imperceptible. The word must describe what sharp eyesight attempts but fails to do with that boundary.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Perceive’: Correct. The sharpest sight cannot perceive (make out) the border line because the blending is so perfect. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Abbreviate’: Wrong. "Define" is a conceptual act, not a visual one; the blank calls for a word about seeing. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Restate’: Wrong. The poem is saying vision fails to detect the line, not that it chooses to look away. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Explain’: Wrong. Reflecting is what the water does; it doesn't describe what vision fails to do. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
